title: Bioanalytics for Engineers - Homework Assignment

cell_lines: GFP-Proteins A, B, C, D and E

questions.prompt: Effect of pathway activation on Proteins A-E: Location and Phosphorylation
Suggest the most likely order for Proteins A-E within this pathway
At which point within the pathway does the inhibitor act?
Describe the general rationale by which you ordered the signal transduction pathway

free_text_rubric: Q2b: [2 pts] Downstream of Protein D and upstream of or inhibiting Protein E.

Q2c: Rationale for Protein B (1 pt): [0.5 pt] Correctly identifies Protein B as first component in pathway; [0.5 pt] Justifies by stating it is located at plasma membrane, implying it is ligand's receptor.

Q2c: Rationale for Protein C (1 pt): [0.5 pt] Correctly places Protein C after Protein B; [0.5 pt] Justifies this by describing its translocation from cytoplasm to plasma membrane upon ligand activation.
Q2c: Rationale for Protein A (1 pt): [0.5 pt] Correctly identifies Protein A as final protein component; [0.5 pt] Justifies by describing its translocation from cytoplasm to nucleus.

Q2c: Rationale for Ordering D & E (2 pts): [1 pt] Correctly describes Western Blot results for both proteins in the presence of the inhibitor (D is still phosphorylated, E is not); [1 pt] Correctly concludes from this evidence that Protein D is upstream of Protein E.
